binaries.title: fsdepends.sys - Cross-branch recompilation diff; no security-relevant change in DependentFSCheckFileHandle

changed_functions.name: DependentFSCheckFileHandle
key_changes: Dependency-pointer validation is present in BOTH builds: after loading a dependency entry pointer (mov rdx, [rcx+rax*8+0x10]), both builds execute 'cmp rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]; jnz <skip>' before updating the match counter. Unpatched at 0x1C0008808-0x1C0008812, patched at 0x1C0008827-0x1C0008831. There is no missing-validation gap between the builds.

Output-buffer-size checks against 0x28 and 0x10 on IoStatusBlock.Information are equivalent in both builds; both bail out to the STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL (0xC00000C3) error/trace return path when the size is too small. The only difference is code generation: cmovb+js (client branch) vs jnb+fall-through (server branch).
Remaining differences are register allocation, instruction selection, and relocated jump/data targets caused by recompilation across two Windows servicing branches (19041 vs 20348).
changed_functions.security_notes: DependentFSCheckFileHandle is an exported function of fsdepends.sys that determines whether a file handle / FILE_OBJECT on a virtual storage volume (VHD, cloud-file placeholder) has file-system dependencies. It queries object name, file and volume information, RtlIsCloudFilesPlaceholder, acquires the driver dependency table under an ERESOURCE, obtains the filter instance context, builds a dependency list via DepFSGenerateDependencyList, then walks the list releasing volume/dependency context references. In the final walk, each entry's stored context pointer is compared against the acquired instance context (cmp rdx, [Context]; jnz) before the match count is updated, and this validation exists identically in both builds. The output-buffer-size checks also exist in both builds. No use-after-free, authorization-context confusion, or missing check is introduced or removed by this diff.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED dependency walk (0x1C00087F4):
  movzx   edx, word ptr [rcx+rax*8+8]
  test    r15b, dl
  jz      loc_1C0008823
  mov     rdx, [rcx+rax*8+10h]        ; load entry context pointer
  cmp     rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]     ; validate against acquired instance context
  jnz     loc_1C0008819               ; skip count update if not matching
  movzx   esi, word ptr [rcx+rax*8+0Ah]

PATCHED dependency walk (0x1C0008813):
  movzx   edx, word ptr [rcx+rax*8+8]
  test    r15b, dl
  jz      loc_1C0008842
  mov     rdx, [rcx+rax*8+10h]        ; load entry context pointer
  cmp     rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]     ; validate against acquired instance context
  jnz     loc_1C0008838               ; skip count update if not matching
  movzx   esi, word ptr [rcx+rax*8+0Ah]

Buffer size check UNPATCHED (0x1C000838C): cmp [rsp+130h+IoStatusBlock.Information], 0x28 ; cmovb ebx,eax ; js loc_1C000892F (WPP trace + STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL return).
Buffer size check PATCHED (0x1C0008384): cmp [rsp+130h+IoStatusBlock.Information], 0x28 ; jnb loc_1C00083C7 ; else mov ebx,0xC00000C3 fall into trace/return. Semantically equivalent.

changed_functions.name: DepFSPostVolumeMount (sub_1C0001810)
key_changes: IRQL read changed from direct control-register read (mov rax, cr8 at 0x1C0001A0E) to the exported helper call KeGetCurrentIrql. The IRQL is used to decide whether to run dependency enumeration synchronously or queue it as a work item.
Lock resource and event-object data addresses relocated (data layout shift).
changed_functions.security_notes: Correctness/portability code-generation change. Both instruction sequences obtain the current IRQL; no validation or trust boundary changes.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED 0x1C0001A0E: mov rax, cr8
PATCHED  0x1C0001A0E: call cs:__imp_KeGetCurrentIrql

changed_functions.name: DepFSRegisterFilters (sub_1C000D1A8)
key_changes: FLT_REGISTRATION.Flags value differs between builds (feature/compat flag).
FLT_REGISTRATION callback pointers and telemetry data addresses relocated (recompilation).
changed_functions.security_notes: Registration flag/feature difference and data-address relocation. Not a security fix.

changed_functions.name: DepFSGenerateDependencyList (sub_1C000DDD0)
key_changes: Control-flow layout of the dependency-enumeration loop differs.
Error propagation path reorganized.
Underlying allocation, FltGet*, and refcount operations unchanged.
changed_functions.security_notes: Recompilation restructuring of the same logic; no added or removed security check. Function is at 0x1C000DDD0 in the unpatched build and relocated to 0x1C000DD50 in the patched build.

changed_functions.name: memset (sub_1C0002180)
key_changes: Library memset re-vectorized with a different SIMD block-size strategy.

changed_functions.name: WPP_SF_qddZ (sub_1C00015CC)
key_changes: WPP software-trace helper; argument-marshalling order differs.

changed_functions.name: DepFSForEachDependency (sub_1C000B7D0)
key_changes: Allocation-failure error path reorganized.
Call targets relocated (recompilation).

changed_functions.name: DepFSCheckDependencies
key_changes: Different implementations between branches. Unpatched (client, 0x1C000E7C8) is a thin ExAllocatePoolWithTag/DepFSSendIoctl retry wrapper returning the IOCTL status. Patched (server, 0x1C000E6E4) takes additional arguments and, after the query IOCTL succeeds and reports at least 0x38 bytes (if (v18 >= 0x38)), iterates the returned dependency entries.

In the patched loop, for each non-NULL entry whose signature word equals 0x3030 (if (v11 != 0 && *(_WORD *)v11 == 12336)) it calls DepFSIncrementActiveMountCount, checks the entry's in-use fields, then in a second pass calls DepFSDecrementActiveMountCount (only on failure) and DependentFSDereferenceDependency under the same NULL guard. The unpatched build has none of this bookkeeping.
changed_functions.security_notes: Active-mount-count bookkeeping present only in the server branch, not a delivered security fix. No shared code path gains or loses a validation check, and every per-entry pointer dereferenced in the patched loop is NULL-checked in the build that contains it.

changed_functions.name: DepFSInstanceSetup (sub_1C000ED80)
key_changes: Minor restructuring and data-address relocation.

changed_functions.name: DepFSInstanceQueryTeardown (sub_1C000FE20)
key_changes: Error path reorganized; lock resource address relocated.

changed_functions.name: DepFSPreVolumeMount (sub_1C000B330)
key_changes: FltAllocateGenericWorkItem hoisted earlier; error path reorganized.

changed_functions.name: DepFSHandleQueryDependentVolume (sub_1C000BA18)
key_changes: Data-address relocation.

changed_functions.name: DepFSBugCheckNotEnoughSpace (sub_1C0001188)
key_changes: Variable-initialization order and buffer handling reorganized.

changed_functions.name: DepFSPreCreate (sub_1C0007010)
key_changes: Register allocation (r9 to r8) and data/call-target relocation.
changed_functions.security_notes: Register allocation and address relocation only.

changed_functions.name: DepFSRegisterFilters (sub_1C000D1A8)
key_changes: FLT_REGISTRATION.Flags value differs; callback pointers relocated.

changed_functions.name: DepFSCheckSmbLoopback (sub_1C0008E18)
key_changes: Field-init order and data-address relocation.

changed_functions.name: DepFSDismountDependencyList (sub_1C000A5B0)
key_changes: Data-address and lock-variable relocation.

changed_functions.name: DepFSPreShutdown (sub_1C000AC10)
key_changes: Call-target relocation.

changed_functions.name: DepFSPostVolumeMount (sub_1C0001810)
key_changes: IRQL read changed from mov rax, cr8 to call KeGetCurrentIrql.
Lock resource and event-object data addresses relocated.
changed_functions.security_notes: IRQL read method change; correctness/portability, not security.

security_findings.entry_point: Exported kernel function (0x1C0008070) reachable via file open/create operations on virtual storage volumes (VHD, cloud-file placeholders) routed through the filter stack
call_chain: User-mode file operation on VHD/cloud file
Dependency-list walk with per-entry context validation
security_findings.vulnerability_class: None identified in this diff (cross-branch recompilation)
security_findings.description: DependentFSCheckFileHandle determines whether a file handle / FILE_OBJECT on a virtual storage volume has file-system dependencies. Both builds perform the same per-entry context-pointer validation in the dependency-list walk (mov rdx, [rcx+rax*8+0x10]; cmp rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]; jnz skip) before updating the match counter, and both enforce the same output-buffer-size checks against 0x28 and 0x10 on IoStatusBlock.Information, bailing to the STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL error path when the size is too small. The observed differences between the two binaries are recompilation and code-generation artifacts (instruction selection such as cmovb+js vs jnb+fall-through, register allocation, and relocated jump/data targets) arising from the two builds belonging to different Windows servicing branches (client 19041 vs server 20348). No missing pointer validation, use-after-free, or authorization-context confusion is introduced or removed.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable. No reachable vulnerability is introduced or removed by this diff. The dependency-pointer validation and output-buffer-size checks are present in both builds.
security_findings.evidence:
Dependency walk present identically in both builds.

UNPATCHED (0x1C0008808):
  mov     rdx, [rcx+rax*8+10h]
  cmp     rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]
  jnz     loc_1C0008819

PATCHED (0x1C0008827):
  mov     rdx, [rcx+rax*8+10h]
  cmp     rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]
  jnz     loc_1C0008838

Buffer-size check UNPATCHED (0x1C000838C): cmp [rsp+130h+IoStatusBlock.Information], 0x28 ; cmovb ebx,eax ; js loc_1C000892F (WPP trace + STATUS_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL return).
Buffer-size check PATCHED (0x1C0008384): cmp [rsp+130h+IoStatusBlock.Information], 0x28 ; jnb loc_1C00083C7 ; else mov ebx,0xC00000C3 into trace/return.
security_findings.assembly:
Dependency-list walk, both builds:
  cmp     [rcx+4], esi
  jbe     <end of walk>
  mov     eax, ebx
  add     rax, rax
  movzx   edx, word ptr [rcx+rax*8+8]     ; entry flags
  test    r15b, dl
  jz      <else branch: test dl,2 / DereferenceDependencyContext>
  mov     rdx, [rcx+rax*8+10h]            ; entry context pointer
  cmp     rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]         ; validate against acquired instance context
  jnz     <skip match-count update>
  movzx   esi, word ptr [rcx+rax*8+0Ah]   ; match count updated only on equality
  mov     rcx, rdx
  call    DereferenceVolumeContext

IRQL read helper change in DepFSPostVolumeMount (sub_1C0001810):
  UNPATCHED 0x1C0001A0E: mov rax, cr8
  PATCHED  0x1C0001A0E: call cs:__imp_KeGetCurrentIrql

breakpoints.why: Function entry. Arguments: rcx = file handle, rdx = FILE_OBJECT*, r8d = flags, r9 = pointer to a mode word, [rsp+0x28] = optional out int*. Present in both builds.

Instruction 'mov rdx, [rcx+rax*8+0x10]' followed by 'cmp rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]; jnz'. The per-entry context validation is present in both builds; compare rdx to [rsp+130h+Context] to observe the match/skip decision.
IRQL read: 'mov rax, cr8' in the client-branch build vs 'call KeGetCurrentIrql' in the server-branch build.

watch: [rsp+130h+Context] = acquired filter instance context (from FltGetInstanceContext); each dependency entry's pointer is compared to it in both builds
[rsp+130h+IoStatusBlock.Information] = queried size, compared against 0x28 (40) and 0x10 (16) in both builds
rcx+rax*8+0x10 = dependency entry context pointer; rcx+rax*8+0x08 = entry flags word; rcx+0x4 = entry count
dl register = flag byte for current dependency entry
key_offsets: 0x1C0008070 = DependentFSCheckFileHandle entry (exported, present in both builds)
0x1C0008808 (unpatched) / 0x1C0008827 (patched) = 'mov rdx, [rcx+rax*8+0x10]; cmp rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]; jnz' validation present in both builds
0x1C000838C (unpatched, cmovb) / 0x1C0008384 (patched, jnb) = output-buffer-size check against 0x28; equivalent bail-out in both builds
0x1C0001A0E = IRQL read: 'mov rax, cr8' (unpatched) vs 'call KeGetCurrentIrql' (patched) in DepFSPostVolumeMount
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable; no vulnerability is introduced or removed. To exercise the path for study, mount a VHD or interact with a cloud-file placeholder and perform file open/create operations that route through fsdepends.sys; the dependency walk validates each entry's context pointer against the acquired instance context in both builds.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In both builds, when the dependency-walk breakpoint hits, rdx (loaded from [rcx+rax*8+0x10]) is compared to [rsp+130h+Context] and the match count is updated only on equality; the reference is released via DereferenceVolumeContext regardless. There is no code path in either build that uses a non-matching or stale entry pointer to update the match count.
debugging_aids.notes: Dependency-list entry stride is 0x10 bytes: flags word at offset +0x08, context pointer at offset +0x10, entry count at [base+0x04]. The context-pointer validation and the output-buffer-size checks are present in both binaries. The differences across all 19 changed functions are recompilation and code-generation artifacts between two Windows servicing branches (client 10.0.19041.7417 vs server 10.0.20348.5256), plus the DepFSPostVolumeMount IRQL-read helper change. No CVE is attributed to fsdepends.sys in the associated update metadata.

summary: This diff pairs a client-branch build of fsdepends.sys (10.0.19041.7417) against a server-branch build (10.0.20348.5256). No security-relevant behavioral change is present. DependentFSCheckFileHandle (exported, 0x1C0008070) performs the same per-entry context-pointer validation in its dependency-list walk (mov rdx, [rcx+rax*8+0x10]; cmp rdx, [rsp+130h+Context]; jnz) and the same output-buffer-size checks (against 0x28 and 0x10 on IoStatusBlock.Information) in both builds; the differences are instruction selection (cmovb+js vs jnb), register allocation, and relocated targets. The one substantive instruction change, in DepFSPostVolumeMount (sub_1C0001810), swaps a direct CR8 read for a KeGetCurrentIrql call, a correctness/portability difference. The remaining changed functions differ only by recompilation artifacts. No use-after-free, missing validation, or authorization-context confusion is introduced or removed.
